proven.claim: PROVEN: classify() correctly identifies "sat" when z3 output is exactly one "sat" line.
proven.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P5
; LINE: 32
; PROVEN: classify() correctly identifies "sat" when z3 output is exactly one "sat" line.
; Assert preconditions + code transitions + NEGATION of property → expect unsat.
(declare-const output_line_count Int)
(declare-const output_line_content Int)   ; 0=sat  1=unsat  2=unknown  3=other
(declare-const classify_result Int)       ; 0=sat  1=unsat  2=unknown  3=error

; Precondition: z3 emits a single line containing "sat"
(assert (= output_line_count 1))
(assert (= output_line_content 0))

; Code transition: classify scans backward; single-line output whose content=sat → returns sat
(assert (=> (and (= output_line_count 1) (= output_line_content 0))
            (= classify_result 0)))

; Negation of property: classify does NOT return sat
(assert (not (= classify_result 0)))
(check-sat)

violations.claim: VIOLATION: verifyBlock passes smt2 directly to execSync with no input validation.
violations.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P3
; LINE: 32
; VIOLATION: verifyBlock passes smt2 directly to execSync with no input validation.
; An empty (assertionless) string makes z3 output "sat" (empty theory is trivially satisfiable).
; verifyBlock then returns { result: "sat" } having verified nothing.
(declare-const assert_count Int)          ; (assert ...) forms present in smt2
(declare-const z3_outputs_sat Int)        ; 1 = z3 stdout line is "sat"
(declare-const classify_returns_sat Int)  ; 1 = classify() resolves to sat

; Code transition: no guard on assert_count in verifyBlock — any value accepted
; Code transition: z3 on assertionless input → trivially satisfiable → outputs "sat"
(assert (= assert_count 0))
(assert (= z3_outputs_sat 1))

; Code transition: classify backward scan matches "sat" line → returns sat
(assert (=> (= z3_outputs_sat 1) (= classify_returns_sat 1)))

; Violation: function reports sat while zero assertions were evaluated
(assert (= classify_returns_sat 1))
(assert (= assert_count 0))
(check-sat)

violations.claim: VIOLATION: execSync("z3 -in -T:5", ...) requires the z3 binary to be in PATH.
violations.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P1
; LINE: 32
; VIOLATION: execSync("z3 -in -T:5", ...) requires the z3 binary to be in PATH.
; verifyBlock establishes no such precondition before invoking execSync.
; Missing z3 → execSync throws ENOENT → catch block returns { result: "error" }.
(declare-const z3_available Int)    ; 1 = z3 found in PATH
(declare-const exec_throws Int)     ; 1 = execSync throws on spawn failure
(declare-const function_result Int) ; 0=sat  1=unsat  2=unknown  3=error

; Code: verifyBlock has no guard checking z3_available before calling execSync
; Code transition: absent binary → execSync throws ENOENT
(assert (= z3_available 0))
(assert (=> (= z3_available 0) (= exec_throws 1)))

; Code transition: catch block — no stdout, stderr omits "timeout" → falls through to error
(assert (=> (= exec_throws 1) (= function_result 3)))

; Violation: verifyBlock returns "error" without Z3 ever running — verification promised, not delivered
(assert (= function_result 3))
(assert (= z3_available 0))
(check-sat)

violations.claim: VIOLATION: Two independent timeout mechanisms produce divergent error classifications.
violations.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: [NEW]
; LINE: 32
; VIOLATION: Two independent timeout mechanisms produce divergent error classifications.
; Z3 internal timeout (-T:5 = 5 000 ms): z3 outputs "unknown" to stdout, exits 0.
;   → execSync succeeds → classify("unknown") → { result: "unknown" }  ✓
; execSync timeout (10 000 ms, fires if z3 internal timer fails): sends SIGTERM, process killed.
;   → execSync throws; stdout may be empty; stderr contains "Killed" not "timeout"
;   → catch: stderr.includes("timeout") is FALSE → returns { result: "error" }  ✗
; A genuine timeout is misclassified as an execution error.
(declare-const z3_internal_timeout Int)    ; 1 = z3 -T:5 fired
(declare-const exec_sigterm_fired Int)     ; 1 = execSync 10 000ms SIGTERM fired
(declare-const stderr_has_timeout_str Int) ; 1 = catch sees "timeout" in stderr
(declare-const returned_result Int)        ; 2=unknown  3=error

; Scenario: execSync timeout fires before z3's internal timer
(assert (= exec_sigterm_fired 1))
(assert (= z3_internal_timeout 0))

; Code transition: SIGTERM from Node.js child_process does not write "timeout" to stderr
(assert (=> (= exec_sigterm_fired 1) (= stderr_has_timeout_str 0)))

; Code transition: catch block — stdout empty (killed mid-run), stderr lacks "timeout"
;   → falls through to final return { result: "error" }
(assert (=> (and (= exec_sigterm_fired 1) (= stderr_has_timeout_str 0))
            (= returned_result 3)))

; Violation: a legitimate timeout is reported as "error" instead of "unknown"
(assert (= returned_result 3))
(assert (= exec_sigterm_fired 1))
(check-sat)
